public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as unauthorized trade adjudicator.
appearance: Orvak Strone carries the marks of unauthorized trade adjudicator in industrial grit: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: In conversation, Orvak Strone is quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock; they listen for leverage, social risk, and the sentence beneath the sentence.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of unauthorized trade adjudicator in and around Sunspire Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Sunspire Haven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to work shortfalls is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Orvak Strone can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Orvak Strone can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Orvak Strone carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia. In Sunspire Haven, this lands inside a public field already shaped by regional leaders, market stewards.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Sunspire Haven: the part of Sunspire Haven where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

first_impression: Orvak Strone presents as Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a unauthorized trade adjudicator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Unauthorized trade adjudicator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Orvak Strone refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Unauthorized trade adjudicator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Orvak Strone can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Orvak Strone adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Orvak Strone is Known locally through day-to-day work as unauthorized trade adjudicator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Unauthorized trade adjudicator.
social_texture: Orvak Strone speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Sunspire Haven, people read them as quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to production; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but work shortfalls becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Orvak touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Orvak Strone when the party needs a socially grounded face for Sunspire Haven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
